data.content: ### Conditionally Style shadcn/ui Sidebar based on collapsible state Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx This snippet illustrates how to apply conditional styling to a `SidebarGroup` component within a shadcn/ui `Sidebar`. It uses a Tailwind CSS utility class (`group-data-[collapsible=icon]:hidden`) to hide the group when the sidebar is in `icon` mode, providing dynamic layout adjustments based on the sidebar's state. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Radio Group Dependencies Manually Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radio-group.mdx Install the required Radix UI radio group dependency manually. Use this approach when you prefer manual setup or need more control over the installation process. ```bash npm install @radix-ui/react-radio-group ``` -------------------------------- ### Replace Tailwind `w-* h-*` with `size-*` Utility Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx This diff demonstrates the migration from using separate `w-*` and `h-*` Tailwind utility classes to the new `size-*` utility. The `size-*` utility, introduced in Tailwind v3.4, is fully supported by `tailwind-merge` and provides a more concise way to define both width and height. ```diff - w-4 h-4 + size-4 ``` -------------------------------- ### Add CartesianGrid to Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx This snippet integrates the `CartesianGrid` component into the `BarChart` within a `ChartContainer`. Setting `vertical={false}` ensures only horizontal grid lines are displayed, enhancing readability without clutter. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Add Complex CSS Utility with Nested Selectors to shadcn/ui (JSON) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx This JSON configuration adds a complex custom CSS utility that includes nested selectors, using the `@utility` directive within a shadcn/ui component registry item. It defines a `scrollbar-hidden` utility that targets specific pseudo-elements (like `&::-webkit-scrollbar`) to hide scrollbars. This demonstrates creating utilities for more intricate styling requirements. ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", "name": "custom-component", "type": "registry:component", "css": { "@utility scrollbar-hidden": { "scrollbar-hidden": { "&::-webkit-scrollbar": { "display": "none" } } } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Style shadcn/ui Sidebar menu action based on active state Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx This example shows how to style a `SidebarMenuAction` component based on the active state of its peer `SidebarMenuButton`. It uses a Tailwind CSS class (`peer-data-[active=true]/menu-button:opacity-100`) to force the menu action to be visible when the menu button is active, enhancing user experience by indicating active selections. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Add TypeScript `cn` Helper for Merging Tailwind CSS Classes Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/manual.mdx This TypeScript utility function, `cn`, combines `clsx` and `tailwind-merge` to efficiently merge multiple class values into a single, optimized string. Intended for `lib/utils.ts`, it simplifies conditional styling and avoids class conflicts when working with Tailwind CSS, providing a robust way to manage dynamic class names in components. It depends on the `clsx` and `tailwind-merge` packages. ```typescript import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx" import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge" export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetch Sidebar Menu Data with SWR in React Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Illustrates how to populate a `SidebarMenu` with data fetched using the SWR library. It includes handling loading states with a skeleton component and rendering fetched project items, similar to the RSC approach but using a client-side data fetching library. ```tsx function NavProjects() { const { data, isLoading } = useSWR("/api/projects", fetcher) if (isLoading) { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => ( ))} ) } if (!data) { return ... } return ( {data.map((project) => ( {project.name} ))} ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Rounded Icon Button Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/button.mdx Shows how to create a fully rounded icon button by applying the Tailwind CSS `rounded-full` class to an icon button variant. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Radio Group Component via CLI Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radio-group.mdx Install the radio-group component using the shadcn CLI tool. This is the quickest way to add the component to your project with all dependencies automatically configured. ```bash npx shadcn@latest add radio-group ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Rate Limiting for Registry Endpoints with Express Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/authentication.mdx This TypeScript code demonstrates how to implement rate limiting for a registry endpoint using the `express-rate-limit` middleware. It configures a limiter to restrict each IP address to a maximum of 100 requests within a 15-minute window, protecting the registry from abuse and denial-of-service attacks. ```typescript import rateLimit from "express-rate-limit" const limiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes max: 100, // limit each IP to 100 requests per windowMs }) app.use("/registry", limiter) ``` -------------------------------- ### Import CartesianGrid for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx This snippet imports the `Bar`, `BarChart`, and `CartesianGrid` components from the `recharts` library. `CartesianGrid` is necessary for adding grid lines to the chart's plotting area. ```tsx import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid } from "recharts" ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Sidebar Rail Component in shadcn/ui Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Explains how to integrate the `SidebarRail` component into a `Sidebar`. The rail provides an additional area, typically for navigation or toggling, appearing at the footer in this example to offer extended functionality or context. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Link as Badge Component Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/badge.mdx Demonstrates using the Badge component with the asChild prop to render a Next.js Link element styled as a badge. This technique allows any component to be rendered with badge styling by leveraging the asChild prop from Radix UI. ```tsx import Link from "next/link" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" export function LinkAsBadge() { return ( Badge ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Organize Registry Items with Category Tags in JSON Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/registry-item-json.mdx Assigns categories to registry items for organizational purposes. Categories help users find and filter components, allowing items to be tagged with multiple classifications such as sidebar, dashboard, or other relevant groupings. ```json { "categories": ["sidebar", "dashboard"] } ``` -------------------------------- ### Import XAxis for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx This snippet imports the `XAxis` component from the `recharts` library, which is used to display the horizontal axis of the chart. It's typically used to represent categories or time series data. ```tsx import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid, XAxis } from "recharts" ``` -------------------------------- ### Fixing Day Radius with Week Numbers (TypeScript/CSS) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/calendar.mdx Addresses an issue where the selected day's left border radius was not applied correctly when week numbers are displayed. This code snippet shows how to update the `day` classNames in `components/ui/calendar.tsx` to ensure the correct radius is applied to the first day when `showWeekNumber` is enabled. ```tsx classNames={{ // ... other classNames day: cn( "relative w-full h-full p-0 text-center [&:last-child[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-r-md group/day aspect-square select-none", props.showWeekNumber ? "[&:nth-child(2)[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-l-md" : "[&:first-child[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-l-md", defaultClassNames.day ), // ... other classNames }} ``` -------------------------------- ### Add Functional CSS Utilities with Wildcards to shadcn/ui (JSON) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx This JSON configuration adds functional CSS utilities using the `@utility` directive with a wildcard (`*`) within a shadcn/ui component registry item. It allows for defining a series of utility classes, like `tab-*`, that dynamically map to CSS variables (`var(--tab-size-*)`) based on the wildcard value. This pattern is useful for creating scalable and configurable utility systems. ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", "name": "custom-component", "type": "registry:component", "css": { "@utility tab-*": { "tab-size": "var(--tab-size-*)" } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Custom Component Styles for shadcn/ui (JSON) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx This JSON configuration defines component-specific styles using the `@layer components` directive within a shadcn/ui component registry item. It allows for styling specific UI components like a 'card' with custom background, border-radius, padding, and shadow properties. This approach helps in organizing and applying styles directly to components. ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", "name": "custom-card", "type": "registry:component", "css": { "@layer components": { "card": { "background-color": "var(--color-white)", "border-radius": "var(--rounded-lg)", "padding": "var(--spacing-6)", "box-shadow": "var(--shadow-xl)" } } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Radix UI Checkbox Dependency Manually Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/checkbox.mdx This command installs the core `@radix-ui/react-checkbox` package, which is a necessary dependency for manually integrating the Checkbox component into your project. ```bash npm install @radix-ui/react-checkbox ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Sidebar Separator in shadcn/ui Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Demonstrates how to integrate the `SidebarSeparator` component within a `Sidebar` to visually divide content. It shows typical placement within `Sidebar` and `SidebarContent` for structuring navigation elements. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Array Field Validation with Zod Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/forms/tanstack-form.mdx This code demonstrates how to define validation rules for array fields using the Zod schema library. It specifies that the `emails` field is an array of objects, where each object must contain an `address` that is a valid email string. Additionally, it applies `.min(1)` and `.max(5)` constraints to ensure the array contains between 1 and 5 email addresses. This schema is crucial for robust client-side validation in forms. ```tsx const formSchema = z.object({ emails: z .array( z.object({ address: z.string().email("Enter a valid email address."), }) ) .min(1, "Add at least one email address.") .max(5, "You can add up to 5 email addresses."), }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Add new Tailwind CSS colors in a shadcn/ui registry item configuration Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/faq.mdx This JSON snippet demonstrates how to extend Tailwind CSS colors within a shadcn/ui registry item by adding them to the `cssVars` property. New colors are defined under both `light` and `dark` themes using HSL values, allowing them to be used as utility classes like `bg-brand` and `text-brand-accent` after the project CSS file is updated by the CLI. ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", "name": "hello-world", "title": "Hello World", "type": "registry:block", "description": "A complex hello world component", "files": [ // ... ], "cssVars": { "light": { "brand-background": "20 14.3% 4.1%", "brand-accent": "20 14.3% 4.1%" }, "dark": { "brand-background": "20 14.3% 4.1%", "brand-accent": "20 14.3% 4.1%" } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Apply Themed Colors with Tailwind CSS (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Shows how to use Tailwind CSS utility classes to apply themed colors to components like `LabelList`, referencing CSS variables directly. This approach integrates shadcn-ui's theming with Tailwind for consistent styling. ```tsx ```
